input.background: We have established that Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scores (WAISs) vary according to a Normal distribution with μ=100 and σ=15.
input.question: Let us consider the standard errors of means for SRSs of WAISs with n=1, n=4, and n=16.

output.answer:
- The standard error of the mean when n=1 is SE_{\bar{x}}=\frac{\sigma}{n}=\frac{15}{\sqrt{1}}=15.
- The standard error of the mean when n=4 is SE_{\bar{x}}=\frac{15}{\sqrt{4}}=7.5.
- The standard error of the mean based when n=16 is SE_{\bar{x}}=\frac{15}{\sqrt{16}}=3.75.

WAIS_mean.description: The mean of the Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scores (WAISs) distribution.

WAIS_std_dev.description: The standard deviation of the Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scores (WAISs) distribution.

sample_size_n1.description: Sample size for the first simple random sample (SRS) of WAISs.

sample_size_n4.description: Sample size for the second simple random sample (SRS) of WAISs.

sample_size_n16.description: Sample size for the third simple random sample (SRS) of WAISs.

meta_info.caption: Standard error of the mean and sample size (WAIS).
